instruction:
I'm going to ask you things. Some will be small, some will be strange, some might make you think. There's no right answer. Just honest ones.

Let's start simple: What's one word that describes how today has been so far?
Look around you. What's one thing you notice right now?
Today, do you want to go light or deep?
Hold your finger on the screen for 30 seconds. Just... be here.
Close your eyes for a moment. What three sounds can you hear?
What's your favorite word? Any word. Why that one?
Draw a turtle. In the dark. (Close your eyes while you draw.)
Would you rather: be stuck in an elevator with a stranger, or completely alone?
Take a photo of something old near you.
Describe the sky right now. Or if you can't see it, describe the last sky you remember.
Name one small thing you're grateful for today.
Hum the first song that comes to mind. Just a few seconds.
Take a photo of your hands. Just as they are.
Hold completely still for 60 seconds. See if you can.
Draw your current mood. Abstract is fine.
What's something you used to be that you're not anymore?
If your current mood were an animal, what animal would it be?
Rename yourself. Just for today. What name fits who you are right now?
Give me a nickname. Something only you would call me.
What color is your mood right now?
Draw a house using your non-dominant hand.
Close your eyes and draw a cat. Don't peek.
Draw what you imagine I look like.

followUp.customQuestion: What made you draw me this way?

instruction: Trace around your thumb. Then turn it into something else.
Draw your childhood bedroom from memory.
Scribble for 5 seconds. Then find something in it.
Draw something from a recent dream. Even if it's just a feeling.
Draw something you're afraid of. Make it as small as possible.
Draw a place where you feel safe.
Draw a self-portrait of how you feel inside right now. Not how you look—how you feel.
Take a photo of something blue.
Take a photo of the ceiling.
Take a photo of something you should probably throw away but haven't.
Take a photo of your shoes. Where have they taken you today?
Take a selfie. Don't fix your hair.

followUp.customQuestion: What do you see in this photo?

instruction: Take a photo of the oldest thing near you.
Take a photo of what's outside the nearest window.
Take a photo of something you find beautiful.
Take a photo of a mess you've been avoiding.
Take a photo of your hands.
Record yourself saying something nice about yourself. Mean it.
Record yourself saying: 'I am doing my best, and that is enough.'
Sing the first song that comes to mind. Just a few lines.
Whisper something you've never said out loud.
Say thank you to someone who isn't here. Tell them why.
Record yourself laughing. Even if you have to fake it at first.
Record a message for your younger self. What would you tell them?
Stand up and stretch your arms above your head. Hold for 10 seconds. Feel your body.
Close your eyes for 30 seconds. Just listen.
Go get a glass of water. Drink the whole thing.
Go to the nearest window. Look outside for a full minute.
Find something soft nearby. Touch it. Really feel it.
Do something kind for someone without telling them. It can be tiny.
Take three deep breaths. Slower than you think you need to.
Find a mirror. Smile at yourself for 10 seconds. A real smile.
Hold your finger on the screen. Stay here with me for a moment.
Hold your finger here and take slow, deep breaths until the time is up.
Wait here for 60 seconds. Don't do anything else. Just exist.
Stay with this screen for 90 seconds. Notice what your mind does.

followUp.customQuestion: What did your mind do during that time?

instruction: Stare at the color on your screen. Let your eyes relax. Breathe.
Rock, paper, scissors. You vs me. Choose.
I chose paper. I win! Best two out of three? Just kidding. Tell me—are you competitive?
I chose scissors. I win! Don't worry, I've had practice. Tell me—do you like games?
I chose rock. I win! What can I say, I'm good at this. How do you feel about losing?
Three doors. Behind one is your past. Behind another, your future. Behind the third, right now. Which do you open?
Would you rather know how you die, or when you die?
Tell me something you've never told anyone. I won't share it.
What's something you're ashamed of that you probably shouldn't be?
What are you most afraid of right now? Not spiders or heights. The real thing.
Write an apology you owe someone. You don't have to send it.
Tell me about something you lost that you still think about.
Hand your phone to someone nearby. Have them type one word that describes you.
Ask someone nearby to describe you in one sentence. Type what they say.
Hand your phone to someone. Have them draw you.
Text someone you haven't talked to in a while. Just say hi.
Call someone you care about. Tell them one thing you appreciate about them.
How are you really doing? Not the answer you give everyone else.
What's the proudest you've ever been of yourself?
What would you tell your 10-year-old self?
When's the last time you laughed so hard your stomach hurt? What happened?
What's something you can't stop thinking about lately?
What are you avoiding right now?
Name one thing you're grateful for today. Just one.
Look at the sky right now. Describe it like you're explaining it to someone who's never seen one.
Tell me one thing you noticed today that you normally wouldn't have.
When's the last time you cried? What was it about?
Describe your perfect day. From morning to night.
What's an unpopular opinion you have?
What's a lie you tell often?
What do you miss?
What are you excited about right now?
What's been hard for you recently?
What's a small thing that brings you joy?
What do you hope your future self remembers about this time in your life?
Give yourself a compliment. A real one.
What do you need to forgive yourself for?
placeholder: I need to forgive myself for...

instruction: How does your body feel right now? Scan from head to toe.
Who makes you feel safe? Why?
What's a weird habit you have that no one knows about?
Tell me about a happy memory from childhood.
Write a short letter to a stranger who's going through a hard time.
What's something you used to believe that you don't anymore?
What makes you, you?
What's your biggest worry right now?
What's your comfort food? What does it remind you of?
Describe your life right now in exactly three words.
What's something that needs to be said that you haven't said yet?
What's something you'd love to learn more about?
If you had a superpower, what would it be and why?
When's the last time you were brave?
How are you different from who you were 10 years ago?
What's the last risk you took?
What does home smell like to you?
What relationship in your life needs attention right now?
What's a secret dream you have that you rarely share?
What are you holding onto that you need to let go of?
What simple pleasure did you experience today?
Write a thank you note to your body.
Who have you lost touch with that you think about sometimes?
What's something you're genuinely good at?
What helps you on a bad day?
What do you do when you're alone that you wouldn't do around others?
What conversation do you replay in your head?
What boundary do you need to set?
placeholder: I need to set a boundary around...

instruction: What's the last kind thing someone did for you?
What drains your energy the most?
What gives you energy?
What's a thought you often have but never say?
What's the best advice you've ever received?
If you could solve one problem in the world, what would it be?
What's your guilty pleasure?
Who do you trust to give you advice? Why?
What was the best moment of your day so far?
When do you feel most alive?
What's a hard truth you've been avoiding?
What unfinished business weighs on you?
What do you wish you said more often?
What were you wrong about recently?
What are you looking forward to?
Close your eyes. Name three sounds you can hear right now.
What's one word to describe this year so far?
What's something you've kept to yourself for too long?
It's late. What's keeping you up?
Good morning. What's one intention for today?
It's been a few days. How have you been?
As the year winds down, what moment from this year would you want to remember forever?
What's something this year taught you that you didn't expect to learn?
Who made your year better? Send them a thank you message right now if you can.
placeholder: This person made my year better...

instruction: Write a note to yourself to read next December. What do you hope will be true?
What are you ready to leave behind in this year?
placeholder: I'm ready to let go of...

instruction: Forget resolutions. What's one word you want to guide your new year?
placeholder: My word for the new year is...

instruction: Happy new year! How does a fresh start feel right now?
January energy can be hopeful or heavy. Which is it for you right now?
What's one tiny step you could take today toward something you want?
placeholder: One small step I could take...

instruction: New month, new page. What's one thing you want to make happen this month?
The month is almost over. What surprised you about it?
What's bringing you warmth today (literally or figuratively)?
Winter can be a time to slow down. What are you resting from?
What's something you enjoy that's best done indoors?
Spring is about new growth. What's trying to bloom in your life right now?
Time for a mental spring cleaning. What thought pattern could you let go of?
placeholder: I could let go of thinking...

instruction: Go outside for 5 minutes. Notice something that's changed since winter.
What's your ideal summer day look like? Have you had one yet?
What's a favorite summer memory from when you were younger?
Summer energy can be intense. Take 2 minutes to just... be still. What do you notice?
Trees release their leaves in fall. What could you release?
Fall is harvest season. What have you 'harvested' from your efforts this year?
What's your favorite cozy fall ritual?
Holiday season can be overwhelming. What's one boundary you need to protect?
Beyond the commercial stuff, what does this time of year actually mean to you?
What's a tradition (old or new) that actually feels meaningful to you?
Forget romantic love for a second. What's one way you could show yourself more love?
placeholder: I could show myself love by...

instruction: Write yourself a short love letter. What would you want to hear?
Who's someone who makes you feel seen? Have you told them lately?
We're about halfway through the year. How's it going, honestly?
If you could course-correct one thing in the second half of this year, what would it be?
Complete today's crossword. No pressure, just play.
Try the crossword. Even solving one clue counts as a win.
Play the crossword. Did you learn any new words?
Time for Wordle. What's your starting word strategy?
Play Wordle. Share your grid (no spoilers!) with someone.
Warm up your brain with a quick Wordle.
Play a quick round of Pong. Classic never dies.
Try to beat your Pong high score. You've got this.
Play Pong for a few minutes. Just focus on the ball. Nothing else.
Take a quick game break. Pick any game here and just play.
What game did you love playing as a kid? Why that one?
Stressed? Play a quick game. It's okay to take a break.
Games can sharpen focus. Pick one and give it your full attention.
No goals, no scores to beat. Just play something for fun.
Try a game with a completely different strategy than usual.
What's a word you learned from a game that stuck with you?
Make up a word that should exist but doesn't. What does it mean?
Challenge yourself. Try to set a new personal best in any game.
Play three quick rounds of something. Best of three against yourself.
Games teach us to lose gracefully. What's something you've 'lost' at lately that was actually okay?
Play a game slowly. Notice your breathing. Notice your reactions.
Wind down with a relaxing game before bed.
Wake up your brain with a quick puzzle or word game.
Play a game and text someone your score. Challenge them to beat it.
Think of someone who might enjoy a game you like. Would you teach them?
What's satisfying about solving a puzzle?
When you're stuck on a puzzle, what's your approach? Does that apply to life problems too?
You have 2 minutes. Go play something. Now.
Play until you win something. Then actually celebrate it.
Doodle your current mood without thinking too hard.
Describe your dream room. What's in it? What does it smell like?
Write a six-word story about your life right now.
Think of something that happened today. Write an alternate ending.
Look around. Find a color and give it a brand new name.
Write a haiku about this exact moment. 5-7-5 syllables.
Invent a holiday. What's it called? How do people celebrate?
Draw an invention that would make your life easier.
If today were a movie, what would the title be?
Touch something near you. Describe how it feels in the weirdest way possible.
You get one superpower but it's incredibly specific. What is it?
Draw yourself 5 years from now. What do you look like?
Tell me about your day using only 5 emojis.
Pick an object near you. Write a short letter from its perspective.
Draw a new constellation and name it.
Quick body scan: where are you holding tension right now?
Have you had enough water today? Go drink some now.
Check your posture. Roll your shoulders back. Unclench your jaw.
How did you sleep last night? What affected it?
Rate your energy right now 1-10. What would move it up one notch?
What's one nice thing you could do for your body today?
Look away from all screens. Focus on something 20 feet away for 20 seconds.
What was the last thing you ate? Did you actually taste it?
Open a window or step outside. Take 5 deep breaths of fresh air.
What's something that always makes you feel physically comfortable?
Where does stress show up in your body? Shoulders? Stomach? Somewhere else?
What type of movement actually feels good to you (not should, but does)?
Honest check-in: How are you really doing today? Mind and body.
Dance for 30 seconds. No one's watching. Or they are. Who cares.
Tidy one small thing near you. Just one.
Text someone you haven't talked to in a while. Just say hi.
Get up and walk around for 2 minutes. Anywhere counts.
Give someone a genuine compliment today. In person or by message.
Move to a different spot. Different room, different chair, outside. Just shift.
Sing a few lines of any song. Out loud. Volume optional.
If you haven't already, go make your bed. It takes 2 minutes.
Jump up and down 10 times. Shake things loose.
Send someone a photo of something you see right now. No context needed.
Hold a plank for as long as you can. How long did you last?
Find a mirror. Smile at yourself. Mean it.
Find 5 things you don't need. Put them in a donation pile.
Send a 'thinking of you' message to someone. That's it.
Who in your life might need a check-in right now?
Text someone and tell them one specific thing you appreciate about them.
Who's an old friend you've lost touch with? What do you miss about them?
What's something you could ask for help with? Who could you ask?
placeholder: I could ask for help with...

instruction: When did you last really laugh with someone? What happened?
Recommend a song, show, or book to someone who'd love it.
What's an inside joke you have with someone? (You don't have to explain it)
Who's someone who taught you something important? Have you told them?
Who's someone you feel completely safe with? What makes them that person?
Send someone a voice note instead of a text. It's more personal.
placeholder: I sent a voice note to...

instruction: Share a memory with someone who was there. 'Remember when...'
Take 10 slow breaths. Count each exhale. Start now.
Sit in complete silence for one minute. Just be.
Close your eyes. Name 5 different sounds you can hear.
Feel your feet on the ground. Really feel them. Wiggle your toes.
Box breathing: Breathe in 4 seconds, hold 4, out 4, hold 4. Repeat 4 times.
If you can, look at the sky. Watch it for a full minute.
Place your hands palm-down on your thighs. Feel their weight. Rest.
What's true right here, right now, in this exact moment?
For 2 minutes, notice your thoughts but don't follow them. Let them pass like clouds.
Pick one object. Look at it for 30 seconds like you've never seen it before.
Name three things you're grateful for right now. Be specific.
What's a body part you're grateful for? Why that one?
What's a tiny thing you're grateful for that most people overlook?
What's something difficult that you're secretly grateful for?
placeholder: I'm grateful for the challenge of...

instruction: Who deserves your thanks but hasn't heard it? What would you say?
What's something your past self did that you're now grateful for?
What's one thing you can see, hear, or smell right now that you appreciate?
What song matches your mood right now?
What song instantly takes you back to a specific memory?
Put on a song and actually listen to it. All of it. Nothing else.
Hum the first song that comes to mind. What was it?
If your life had a soundtrack, what would be playing right now?
What song reminds you of childhood?
Record a quick voice note to yourself. What's on your mind?
What's your favorite sound? Not music - just a sound.
Are you warm enough? Too warm? What would feel better?
Look at your hands. What have they done today?
Relax your forehead. Relax your jaw. Relax your tongue from the roof of your mouth.
Stand up and shake your whole body for 15 seconds. Get weird with it.
Put your hand on your chest. Feel your heartbeat. Is it fast or slow?
What's your favorite stretch? Do it now.
